---
title: "Andrew S. Rosenberg"
subtitle: 'Associate Professor of Political Science<br>University of Florida'
pagetitle: "Andrew S. Rosenberg"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: assets/headshot.webp
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:andrewrosenberg@ufl.edu
    - icon: person-vcard
      text: CV
      href: assets/asr-cv.pdf
    - icon: orcid
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9374-7741
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=pQSgOXsAAAAJ&hl=en
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrew-rosenberg-b7ba1b83/
toc: false
---

I am Associate Professor and Director of Graduate Studies in the [Department of Political Science](https://politicalscience.ufl.edu/) at the University of Florida.

I study race and racism in international politics, global inequality, migration policy, forced displacement, and international order. My published and forthcoming work appears in the *American Journal of Political Science*, *The Journal of Politics*, *International Studies Quarterly*, *Political Analysis*, and elsewhere.

My first book ([order here!](https://press.princeton.edu/books/hardcover/9780691238739/undesirable-immigrants)), *Undesirable Immigrants: Why Racism Persists in International Migration*, was published by Princeton University Press in its International History and Politics series in August 2022. The book received a 2023 Best Book Award from the Race, Ethnicity, and Politics section of the American Political Science Association. It was recently profiled in [*Mother Jones*](https://www.motherjones.com/politics/2022/08/the-chinese-exclusion-act-may-be-in-the-past-but-racism-still-drives-most-immigration-policies/). I also talked about it on the [*New Books in Political Science* podcast](https://newbooksnetwork.com/undesirable-immigrants).

I am completing a second book, [*Hierarchies of Worth: Race, Scarcity, and the Politics of Costly Action*](books/hierarchies.qmd), which argues that international order distributes not only power and rights, but also the political costs states are willing to bear for different populations.

I received a Ph.D. from the Ohio State University, an M.Sc. from the London School of Economics in International Relations (Research), and a B.A. from the Johns Hopkins University in International Studies and Political Science. I am a proud native of Des Moines, Iowa: [one of the coolest cities in the world](https://www.politico.com/magazine/story/2016/01/how-des-moines-iowa-got-cool-213552).

::: {.prospie-note}
*Prospective Ph.D. and M.A. applicants: please see the [Prospective students](contact.qmd#prospective-students) section on my Contact page before emailing.*
:::

## Recent

- **June 2026.** *Reliable Panel Regression: A Default Workflow for Slow-Moving, Mismeasured Variables* — new [working paper](https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/s7fwa_v1), with companion R package [`ferobust`](https://github.com/asrosenberg/ferobust).
- **May 2026.** *Scarcity, Strategy, and the Racialized Politics of Refugee Admission* conditionally accepted at *The Journal of Politics*. [[preprint](https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/fm8u4_v1)]
- **September 2025.** *Traces of the Past* (with Hannah Alarian and Michael Bernhard) wins Best Paper Award, APSA European Politics and Society section.
- **September 2024.** *Barrier or Bridge? Border Walls' Varied Impacts on Terrorist Group Diffusion* (with Nazli Avdan) [published](https://doi.org/10.1177/07388942241270927) in *Conflict Management and Peace Science*.

::: {.photo-credit}
Headshot by [Keely Rosenberg Photography](https://keelyrosenbergphotography.com/).
:::
